ABSI.title: Micron To Rally More Than 57%? Here Are 10 Top Analyst Forecasts For Thursday

MSTR.title: Bitcoin May Be Undervalued, But AI Remains The Biggest Opportunity, Grayscale Exec Says

NUVB.title: H.B. Fuller, Trip.com, Hertz Global And Other Big Stocks Moving Lower In Thursday Pre-Market Session

macro_events.event_summary: May/June PCE inflation prints above 4% — hotter than Fed target — but collapsing crude oil prices are expected to feed through to lower headline CPI/PCE in coming months.
primary_drivers: Sticky core inflation above 4% delays Fed cuts / keeps real rates higher for longer
Falling crude oil = direct headline disinflation via energy CPI component and lower input costs for transport/chemicals
Mixed signal: bearish for energy producers, bullish for energy consumers and rate-sensitive disinflation beneficiaries on the lag

beneficiaries.rationale: Jet fuel is ~25-30% of Southwest's operating costs and it hedges less than peers; a sustained crude decline drops unit costs straight to EBIT. Direct cost-input exposure.
Fuel is the second-largest cost line at Delta (~20%+ of opex). Falling crude directly expands margins and Delta has explicitly guided fuel cost sensitivity in prior quarters.
Ethane/naphtha feedstocks track crude; lower oil compresses input costs for commodity chemicals while end-product prices lag, widening spreads.
Lower diesel/gasoline reduces both Walmart's trucking distribution costs and frees up low-income consumer wallet share at the pump — dual tailwind from the oil leg of the story.
Diesel is a major variable cost; falling crude lowers fuel surcharges lag but improves underlying margin. Direct input cost exposure.

losers.rationale: Upstream realizations move directly with crude; 'tumbling oil prices' compresses earnings per barrel. Largest US integrated, most index-weighted casualty.
Permian-heavy pure-play E&P with high oil beta and leveraged balance sheet — earnings and FCF highly sensitive to WTI; downside leverage to falling crude.
Oilfield services demand follows E&P capex, which contracts when oil falls. North America completions activity is the most price-sensitive segment Halliburton dominates.

headlines: U.S. inflation tops 4%, PCE shows, but tumbling oil prices to bring price relief soon

macro_events.event_summary: Bitcoin has crashed to multi-year lows, with ethereum also selling off sharply.
primary_drivers: Direct mark-to-market losses on crypto holdings and crypto-denominated revenue
Lower trading volumes and transaction fees on crypto platforms
Reduced demand for mining hardware/hosting and collapse in mining economics

beneficiaries.rationale: Equity/options trading volumes tend to pick up share of wallet when crypto craters as retail rotates back into stocks; however crypto is ~15-20% of HOOD revenue so net effect is mixed — listed as low-confidence relative beneficiary on rotation, not absolute.

losers.rationale: MicroStrategy holds hundreds of thousands of BTC on balance sheet; equity trades as a leveraged proxy on bitcoin price. Multi-year BTC lows directly impair NAV and could trigger convertible debt concerns.
Coinbase revenue is ~majority transaction fees tied to crypto trading volume and price levels; bear market crushes both volumes and custody AUM fees. Direct earnings hit.
Marathon Digital is a pure-play BTC miner — revenue is BTC mined × BTC price. At multi-year lows many miners go cash-flow negative, especially post-halving, raising dilution/solvency risk.
Same mechanism as MARA — pure-play bitcoin miner with high fixed power/hosting costs; sustained low BTC prices push hashprice below breakeven for most US miners.
CleanSpark is a pure-play bitcoin miner with direct revenue exposure to BTC spot price; multi-year lows compress margins and may force equity raises.
Robinhood's crypto trading line (~15-25% of transaction revenue depending on quarter) collapses on volume and price drawdown. Direct revenue hit even if equities offset partially.
Hut 8 miner with BTC treasury holdings — double exposure via mining revenue and balance-sheet BTC marks.

headlines: Bitcoin and ethereum prices today, Thursday, June 25, 2026: Bitcoin hits its lowest levels in years

macro_events.event_summary: Micron's earnings beat eased fears about AI demand sustainability, lifting tech stocks broadly, while oil prices continued to decline.
primary_drivers: AI memory/HBM demand validation read-through to semis
Lower oil prices reduce input costs and ease inflation concerns
Risk-on rotation into AI capex beneficiaries

beneficiaries.rationale: Direct subject of the earnings beat — HBM3E and DRAM pricing/demand confirmed strong, validating the AI memory cycle.
Closest memory/storage peer to Micron — NAND pricing and AI storage demand commentary from MU reads directly across to Western Digital.
MU's HBM sell-through confirms Nvidia GPU end-demand is robust; HBM is gated supply for H100/B200, so MU strength validates Nvidia's order book.
Memory capex commentary from MU drives WFE (wafer fab equipment) orders; Applied Materials has highest memory equipment exposure among large-cap semicap.
Lam Research is heavily levered to memory etch/deposition capex; MU guidance on fab spend is the most direct leading indicator.
Falling oil cuts jet fuel costs — Delta has historically high fuel-cost sensitivity and limited hedging vs peers.

losers.rationale: Continued oil price decline directly compresses upstream realizations; Exxon is the most-traded proxy for crude weakness.
Pure-play US shale producer with high oil-price beta and leveraged balance sheet — among most sensitive large-caps to falling crude.
Oilfield services demand softens when crude falls and E&P capex guidance gets cut; Schlumberger is the bellwether.
Small/mid-cap E&P with high oil-price leverage; falls disproportionately on continued crude weakness.

headlines: Tech stocks surge as Micron earnings ease AI fears, oil falls further

macro_events.event_summary: Core PCE inflation hit a 3-year high, reviving discussion of a possible Fed rate hike rather than cuts.
primary_drivers: Higher-for-longer / rising rate expectations lifting front-end yields and the dollar
Discount rate compression on long-duration equities
Net interest margin tailwind for asset-sensitive banks

beneficiaries.rationale: Charles Schwab is highly asset-sensitive; higher front-end rates reduce bank sweep cash drag and lift NIM. Also benefits from reduced fears of cash sorting reversal if rates stay elevated.
Large-cap money-center bank with positive asset sensitivity to short rates; a hawkish repricing of the front end directly lifts NII guidance trajectory.
Higher-for-longer rates extend the refinancing wall and push more issuers to roll debt at higher coupons, driving rating-action volume; Moody's revenue is rate-volatility sensitive.
Renewed rate-hike debate spikes SOFR/Fed Funds futures and Treasury futures volumes — CME's highest-margin complex. Direct revenue link to rate-path uncertainty.
Sticky inflation with the Fed losing credibility on the 2% target is a classic gold tailwind; Newmont is the most liquid US-listed pure-play gold miner with operating leverage to spot gold.
Energy is one of the few sectors with positive earnings revisions in a sticky-inflation regime; also a real-asset hedge with shareholder return policy tied to cash flows, not multiple expansion.

losers.rationale: Homebuilders depend on mortgage rates; renewed hike talk pushes 10Y and 30Y mortgage rates back up, killing affordability and cancellation rates worsen. D.R. Horton is the entry-level pure-play.
Zillow's transaction-volume model is directly tied to existing home sales, which collapse when mortgage rates spike. Hawkish surprise = lower turnover = lower Premier Agent and mortgage revenue.
High-multiple, long-duration growth name trading on terminal value; among the most rate-sensitive in the AI-momentum cohort given >60x forward sales.

Realty Income — large-cap net-lease REIT with long-duration cash flows and AFFO growth dependent on spread investing. Higher rates compress investment spreads and the dividend-yield comp gets less attractive vs. T-bills.

headlines: Fed's preferred inflation measure hits three-year high, keeping talk of possible rate hike in play

macro_events.event_summary: May PCE inflation came in hot at over 4.0% YoY with consumer spending remaining strong, signaling sticky inflation and pushing back expectations for Fed rate cuts.
primary_drivers: Higher-for-longer rates → higher discount rate on long-duration equities
Net interest margin expansion for banks with asset-sensitive balance sheets
Inflation hedge demand → real assets and pricing-power names
Stronger USD pressure on multinationals with FX translation exposure

beneficiaries.rationale: Energy majors benefit from inflation tailwinds; commodity prices typically embedded in PCE strength, and XOM has demonstrated pricing power with strong free cash flow that re-rates higher in inflationary regimes.
Largest US money-center bank with asset-sensitive balance sheet — delayed Fed cuts keep NIM elevated. Strong consumer spending also reduces credit loss provisioning concerns on card book.
Capital One is a pure-play consumer credit name; 'strong consumer spending' directly boosts card purchase volume and interchange revenue, while higher-for-longer rates support card yield spreads.
Charles Schwab benefits directly from higher-for-longer short rates via money market sweep spreads and reduced cash sorting pressure; rate-cut delay extends NIM tailwind.
Integrated oil major with inflation-linked revenue and high dividend yield acts as inflation hedge; benefits from same commodity pass-through driving PCE prints.

losers.rationale: Zillow revenue tied to housing transaction volume; elevated mortgage rates from hot PCE freeze existing home sales activity, the core of its Premier Agent and mortgage segments.
Realty Income is a long-duration triple-net REIT highly sensitive to the 10-year yield; hot PCE pushing yields up directly compresses cap rate valuations.
Unprofitable mid-cap growth name with no near-term earnings; valuation is pure DCF on out-year cash flows, gets re-rated lower when discount rate stays elevated.

headlines: US PCE inflation measure tops 4.0% in May; consumer spending strong
